explanation: We need to formalize: In PCV and PSV mode, at the end of inspiration, and if inspiratory pause is not required, it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure (RM), if it is required by the GUI. First, 'in PCV and PSV mode' means PCVMode or PSVMode is active. 'At the end of inspiration' is mapped with inspiratoryPhaseEnd. 'If inspiratory pause is not required' is mapped to !inspiratoryPauseButton. 'If required by the GUI' for RM means RMButton is pressed. The action to be possible is 'initiate RM', so RM should be possible to become true. We can express 'if all the above conditions hold, then it should be possible for RM to be initiated if the GUI requires it' as: Globally, (PCVMode or PSVMode) and inspiratoryPhaseEnd and not inspiratoryPauseButton and RMButton implies that in the next step, RM can be true. Thus: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton) -> F RM)

All these are necessary conditions for RM initiation. The atomic propositions PCVMode and PSVMode signal the modes, inspiratoryPhaseEnd signals end of inspiration, inspiratoryPauseButton signals if pause is required, and RMButton signals a GUI request for RM. The absence of inspiratory pause is !inspiratoryPauseButton. The implication becomes: for all times, if ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton), then F RM (i.e., it shall be possible to initiate RM after these conditions). Globally applies, so we wrap with G.

explanation:
The requirement states that, in PCV or PSV mode, at the end of inspiration (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if an inspiratory pause is not required (i.e., !inspiratoryPauseButton), then a lung recruitment maneuver (RM) can be initiated, but only when requested by the GUI (RMButton). We assume 'possible to initiate' means the system shall allow (enable) the RM procedure in such a context. This is a permissibility condition on RM being active, conditional on the system state at specific moments.

Step-by-step:
1. 'In PCV and PSV mode': PCVMode | PSVMode.
2. 'At the end of an inspiration': inspiratoryPhaseEnd.
3. 'If inspiratory pause is not required': !inspiratoryPauseButton.
4. 'it shall be possible to initiate ... RM, if it is required by the GUI': If RMButton, then RM is possible at this point.

This gives us: If (in (PCV or PSV mode) AND at inspiratoryPhaseEnd AND NOT inspiratoryPauseButton), then (RMButton -> RM). This is a state-triggered implication, and we want it to always hold, so it is wrapped with global (G).

Put together: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton) -> (RMButton -> RM))

explanation:
We want to formally express: In PCV or PSV mode, at the end of inspiration, if inspiratory pause is not required, and the GUI requests it, then it shall be possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM). 

First, map each phrase:
- 'In PCV and PSV mode': (PCVMode | PSVMode) is active
- 'at the end of inspiration': inspiratoryPhaseEnd
- 'if inspiratory pause is not required': !inspiratoryPauseButton (i.e., Inspiratory Pause is NOT requested by GUI)
- 'if it is required by the GUI': RMButton (i.e., RM is requested via GUI)
- 'it shall be possible to initiate ... RM': RM (i.e., RM can start)

The natural language describes an implication that should hold at any time this situation occurs, so express with G (globally/always).

The situation: Whenever (PCV or PSV), and at the end of inspiration, and inspiratory pause is not required, and if GUI requests RM, then RM shall be possible (i.e., RM must be able to start at that point). 

So:
G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton) -> F RM)

Here, F RM asserts that eventually RM is started after those conditions hold, which fits "it shall be possible to initiate".

Detailed Explanation Dict:
- 'In PCV and PSV mode' --> 'PCVMode | PSVMode'
- 'at the end of inspiration' --> 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd'
- 'inspiratory pause is not required' --> '!inspiratoryPauseButton'
- 'if it is required by the GUI' --> 'RMButton'
- 'initiate a lung recruitment procedure (RM)' --> 'F RM' (eventually RM)
- Whole implication universally: 'G'
Final LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton) -> F RM)

explanation: First, 'In PCV and PSV mode' means we care only when the system is in either PCVMode or PSVMode. 'At the end of an inspiration' refers to inspiratoryPhaseEnd. 'If inspiratory pause is not required' can be interpreted as 'not inspiratoryPauseButton' (the GUI did not request a pause). 'It shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure, termed Recruitment Maneuver (RM), if it is required by the GUI' states that if RM is required by the GUI (RMButton), it shall be possible to activate RM at this point. We typically interpret 'it shall be possible' as 'if the GUI requests RMButton at this moment, RM can be started' (i.e., RMButton -> F RM, but because it's for 'at the end of an inspiration', it needs to be conditional on this event and restricted to the relevant modes). So, for every moment globally, whenever (PCVMode or PSVMode) and inspiratoryPhaseEnd and not inspiratoryPauseButton, then (RMButton -> F RM), i.e., if RMButton is pressed by the GUI at the end of inspiration (no pause), RM can be initiated. The LTL formula is: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton) -> (RMButton -> F RM))

explanation:
First, we identify the atomic propositions and structure in the sentence:

- The system must be in PCV or PSV mode: (PCVMode | PSVMode)
- At the end of an inspiration: inspiratoryPhaseEnd
- IF inspiratory pause is NOT required: !(inspiratoryPauseButton)
- It is possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) if required by the GUI: (RMButton -> F RM)
  - This reads as: If RMButton is pushed, then eventually RM becomes active.
  
All these conditions are joined as: In PCV or PSV mode, when inspiration ends and inspiratory pause not required, if the GUI requests RM then it is possible that RM happens. 

This can be written as: G( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton -> (RMButton -> F RM) )

"Possible" here is expressed as "if requested, then it is possible to reach RM" (RMButton -> F RM).
The entire rule is wrapped in G to express that this is always the case, whenever the situation arises.

explanation:
We are to formalize: 'In PCV and PSV mode, at the end of an inspiration and if inspiratory pause is not required, it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure, termed Recruitment Maneuver (RM), if it is required by the GUI.'

Let:
- PCVMode: Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode is active
- PSVMode: Pressure Support Ventilation Mode is active
- inspiratoryPhaseEnd: end of inspiration
- !inspiratoryPauseButton: Inspiratory Pause is NOT required
- RMButton: GUI requests Recruitment Maneuver
- RM: Recruitment Maneuver is initiated/possible

The requirement states: Whenever (we are in PCV or PSV mode) AND (inspiratory phase ends) AND (inspiratory pause is NOT required) AND (GUI requires RM), then it SHALL be possible to initiate RM (which we'll interpret as RM is possible immediately at this point). In LTL, that is a global liveness: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton -> F RM), i.e., in every instance where all those conditions are true, eventually RM is possible (here, using F(RM) indicates that RM can be initiated at or following that state).

Stepwise mapping:
- 'PCV and PSV mode': PCVMode | PSVMode
- 'at the end of an inspiration': inspiratoryPhaseEnd
- 'if inspiratory pause is not required': !inspiratoryPauseButton
- 'if it is required by the GUI': RMButton
- 'should be possible to initiate RM': F RM (eventually RM)

All these conditions must hold together for the trigger, so they are combined by &. The entire implication applies globally, so G( ... ).

explanation: This requirement specifies a condition that applies in two modes: PCVMode and PSVMode. At the end of an inspiration (indicated by inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and if inspiratoryPause is NOT required (i.e., !inspiratoryPause), it must be possible to start Recruitment Maneuver (RM), but only if RM is required by the GUI (i.e., RMButton). Translating this: the possibility to initiate 'RM' is expressed as the implication that if PCVMode OR PSVMode holds, AND inspiratoryPhaseEnd, AND no inspiratoryPause, AND RMButton (set by GUI), then RM (recruitment maneuver) must be possible (so implication to F(RM) or X RM, depending on timing, but since it's about being possible to start, we require that RM can be initiated next if the above conditions are met). In LTL, this can be expressed as: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton) -> X RM).

explanation:
The goal is to translate: "In PCV and PSV mode, at the end of an inspiration and if inspiratory pause is not required, it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure, termed Recruitment Maneuver (RM), if it is required by the GUI." 

First, identify the conditions:
- Mode is either PCVMode or PSVMode. Expressed as: PCVMode | PSVMode.
- At the end of inspiration: inspiratoryPhaseEnd.
- Inspiratory pause is not required: !inspiratoryPauseButton (its absence means pause is not set).
- RM (Recruitment Maneuver) is initiated/possible if required by the GUI: RMButton (or similar external trigger). The formula expresses possibility, so when all conditions above are true and RMButton is pressed, initiating RM is admitted/enabled. 

So, 'it shall be possible to initiate' is taken as: whenever all modes and triggers align, if GUI requests RMButton, then RM can be true/allowed. In LTL, the formula is usually written as a global property: G( [preconditions & RMButton] -> F RM ), meaning whenever preconditions hold and GUI triggers RM, RM eventually becomes true. If instantaneous: G( [preconditions & RMButton] -> RM ).

LTL step-by-step:
1. "In PCV and PSV mode" — PCVMode | PSVMode
2. "at the end of an inspiration" — inspiratoryPhaseEnd
3. "if inspiratory pause is not required" — !inspiratoryPauseButton
4. "if it is required by the GUI" — RMButton (RMButton pressed by GUI means RM is required)
5. "it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure (RM)" — implies that if all preconditions and RMButton, then RM can occur (here, permitted instantaneously): [modes & end & not pause & RMButton] -> RM
6. 'Globally' — requires this property to hold at all times: G( [ ... ] -> RM )

if inspiratory pause is not required
if it is required by GUI
it shall be possible to initiate RM

The formula can be understood as: whenever (globally, G) both (PCVModeSelected | PSVModeSelected) are active, and end of inspiration happens, and inspiratory pause is not pressed, then if RM is requested by the button (RMButton), then RM can be started (RM). In LTL this becomes: G(((PCVModeSelected | PSVModeSelected) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton) -> RM).
